raw_consumer_statement:
I am submitting a complaint against American Express regarding a {$500.00} refund that I am owed from a merchant. Despite multiple attempts to resolve this directly with the company, they have refused to provide the funds. 

Initially, American Express sent a check to an address that I had clearly stated was not my correct address. They then told me the refund would be sent via wire transfer. I waited for weeks, repeatedly being told the wire had been initiated, but I never received it. When I contacted my bank, they confirmed that no such transfer had been received. American Express then claimed the refund had not been sent because it was to a business account, even though it was in my name and all prior payments had been made from that account. 

Later, they claimed to have sent another check, but refused to provide tracking information. I have not received this check and have no reason to believe it was actually sent. American Express is now refusing to void the missing check or provide my refund through the original payment method or via wire transfer. 

I am resigning out of the country and urgently require that American Express return my {$500.00}. Their continued refusal to issue the refund constitutes unlawful withholding of my funds and a constructive denial of my refund rights. 

I request that the CFPB investigate this matter and ensure that American Express provides my refund immediately.
